_README: Seed data for MarketWatch demo. ALL company names are fictional. Signals are deliberately INTERCONNECTED so the Strategy agent can connect dots into a non-obvious threat + opportunity. Feed marketing_signals -> Marketing AI, product_signals -> Product AI, sales_signals -> Sales AI. Hold injected_signal back and add it live during the demo to fire the THREAT alert.

your_company.name: Synapse
your_company.positioning: Premium, mobile-first AI notetaker for fast-moving teams

marketing_signals.content: Launched a new brand campaign: 'Recapio for Everyone — powerful AI meeting notes, now free for small teams.' Heavy spend on YouTube and LinkedIn. Messaging shifted away from 'enterprise-grade' toward 'simple, free, for any team.'
Homepage rewritten. Old hero: 'Enterprise meeting intelligence.' New hero: 'Free forever for teams under 10.' Pricing page now leads with the free tier.
Campaign focused entirely on enterprise: 'Klarith — meeting intelligence for the boardroom.' All creative is desktop screenshots and laptop scenes. No mention of mobile anywhere in ads.
Webinar series 'Klarith for Compliance Teams' — doubling down on regulated-industry, desktop-bound enterprise buyers.
Ran a small seasonal promo: '15% off annual plans this month.' No change in positioning or messaging. Routine discounting.
Influencer push: dozens of small-business and creator accounts posting 'Recapio is free now' content. Clearly a coordinated self-serve, bottoms-up acquisition motion.

product_signals.content: Recent reviews trending very positive on ease-of-use and onboarding: 'set up in two minutes,' 'my whole team adopted it instantly.' Sentiment clearly positive.
A minority of reviews note it lacks deep enterprise controls (SSO, granular admin, audit logs). But these complaints are from larger orgs — small teams don't care and rate it highly.

Loud, repeated complaints across reviews about the mobile experience: 'no real mobile app,' 'the phone version is unusable,' 'I can't review notes on the go.' Top feature request by volume is a proper native mobile app.

More mobile complaints: 'great on desktop, useless when I'm traveling.' Users explicitly say they'd switch to a competitor with strong mobile. Desktop sentiment positive, mobile sentiment very negative.
Feature-request board shows 'native iOS/Android app' as the #1 most-upvoted request for two quarters running, still marked 'not planned.'
Mixed but stable reviews. Some praise, some minor gripes about export formats. Nothing trending, no major theme emerging.
Reviewers increasingly mention 'switched from a paid tool because Recapio is free and good enough.' Several name premium competitors they churned away from.

sales_signals.content: Announced a $40M Series B led by a growth fund known for backing freemium, product-led-growth companies. Press release emphasizes 'capturing the long tail of small teams.'
Hiring surge: 12 new open roles, almost all in 'self-serve growth,' 'SMB / velocity sales,' and 'lifecycle marketing.' No enterprise-AE roles. Clear bet on high-volume, low-touch SMB acquisition.
Opened a new APAC entity and posted roles in Singapore and Bangalore for regional self-serve growth — geographic expansion of the SMB land-grab.
Hiring exclusively enterprise field sales and solutions engineers. Roles are all desktop/web platform focused. Zero mobile-engineering hires posted.
Signed a large public-sector contract (announced via case study). Doubling down on big, slow, desktop-bound enterprise deals.
No notable funding, hiring, or expansion activity this period. Headcount flat. Quiet.

injected_signal.content: BREAKING: Recapio just announced a partnership making it the default meeting-notes integration inside a major team-chat platform, pushing its free tier to that platform's entire user base. This is a distribution land-grab aimed straight at the mid-market.
injected_signal._demo_note: Add this live during the demo (after the first sweep) to fire the THREAT alert. It escalates the Recapio down-market threat from 'emerging' to 'urgent' by adding mass distribution to the free-tier + funding + SMB-hiring pattern.

_expected_strategy_output._note: This is the punchline the seed data is engineered to produce — do NOT hardcode it, but use it to sanity-check that the Strategy agent connects the dots.
_expected_strategy_output.threat: Recapio is executing a funded, deliberate down-market land-grab: $40M raised + SMB/self-serve hiring + APAC expansion + a 'free for small teams' campaign + reviewers churning from paid tools. They're coming for Synapse's mid-market from below. Action: defend the mid-market with clear premium value and a small-team on-ramp before the free tier erodes it.
_expected_strategy_output.opportunity: Klarith — a strong enterprise competitor — has a glaring, loudly-requested mobile gap (#1 feature request for two quarters, marked 'not planned,' users threatening to switch) while hiring zero mobile engineers. Synapse is mobile-first. Action: target Klarith's frustrated mobile users with a switch campaign; this is an open flank.
_expected_strategy_output.distractor: Driftwave is noise this period — flat on all fronts. Correctly de-prioritized.
